title: Recognizing three years of Ukraine defending its sovereign territory against the Russian Federation's second unprovoked assault and full-scale invasion.
plain_summary: This resolution is Congress recognizing that Ukraine has been defending itself from Russia for three years. It started when Russia attacked Ukraine on February 24, 2022, without any good reason. The House of Representatives says Russia's actions are wrong and that Russia has been working with North Korea, Iran, and China. Congress wants to show strong support for Ukraine and believes the United States should keep helping Ukraine defend itself.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Foreign Affairs.

sections.title: Resolution Title
sections.text: This resolution recognizes three years of Ukraine defending its own territory against Russia's second unprovoked attack and full-scale invasion.

sections.title: Background Findings
sections.text: On February twenty-fourth, two thousand twenty-two, Russia launched its second unprovoked attack and full-scale invasion against Ukraine. For three years, the Ukrainian people have withstood this full-scale invasion with extraordinary strength. They have faced this challenge despite confronting a military and economy twice the size of their own. They have also had to deal with foreign troops from North Korea and weapons provided by Iran and China. Russia has carried out a brutal war designed to break Ukrainian spirit. Russia has used sexual assault and rape as weapons of war. They have forced Ukrainian children to leave their country. They have targeted energy systems during harsh winter conditions. They have used chemical weapons against Ukrainians. They have shelled homes, schools, and hospitals. They have used cruel war tactics to harm Ukrainian soldiers and civilians. Despite Russia's efforts to destroy Ukraine, the Ukrainian people are rebuilding infrastructure that was destroyed by Russian missiles. They have adapted quickly and cleverly to stay ahead of changing battlefield conditions. They have successfully attacked Russian military assets that provided weapons used against Ukraine. They have held territory inside of Russia. They continue to fight heroically for their country's independence and right to exist as an independent nation. The Ukrainian military has recaptured thousands of miles of territory since the war began. The Kursk offensive was a remarkable demonstration of combined military tactics, effective use of information and intelligence in an almost transparent battlefield, and successful electronic warfare to weaken Russian forces' intelligence and surveillance abilities. Despite North Korean soldiers being sent to Kursk and the use of Iranian-supplied weapons, Ukrainian soldiers continue to defend their territory. They have applied lessons learned in counter-drone operations, electronic warfare, mobility support, and tank-infantry integration. Ukraine has eliminated nearly three thousand six hundred eighty-nine Russian tanks, thousands of armored combat vehicles, five ships, and four hundred fifty-eight smaller craft. Ukraine's soldiers, volunteers, and wartime industrial base have offered the United States valuable insights on producing strategic outputs quickly and effectively to respond to changing battlefield dynamics. The people of Ukraine have continued to work toward reforms to strengthen their democratic governance and improve their economy. The people of Ukraine will continue to fight against Russia's war against their country, including by pursuing their goals and ambitions while honoring Ukrainian history and culture.

sections.title: House Resolution
sections.text: The House of Representatives stands completely with the people of Ukraine as they continue to defend their people and territory in the third year of Russia's unprovoked full-scale invasion against Ukraine. The House condemns Russia's full-scale invasion into Ukraine and its working together with North Korea, Iran, and China to conduct crimes against humanity including by providing soldiers and weapons to use against the Ukrainian people. The House supports the continued working together among key allies and partners of the United States to strengthen Ukraine's military, governance, and economy. The House supports using Russian frozen assets to support Ukraine's wartime needs and the continued application of sanctions against Russia and adversaries of the United States who have provided material and personnel support. The House reaffirms the United States commitment to the Ukrainian people as they fight to preserve their territory, culture, and history from Russia, Iran, and China.
